problem.title: LRU Cache
problem.description: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache.
assumptions: get: return value or -1 if key not found.
put: insert or update; evict LRU key if capacity exceeded.

example.explanation: Capacity 2. After put(3,3), key 2 is evicted (least recently used). Use HashMap + doubly linked list for O(1) ops.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – HashMap + Doubly Linked List [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Combine a HashMap (for O(1) key lookup) with a doubly linked list (for O(1) ordered insertion/deletion). The most-recently-used node is kept at the head and the least-recently-used at the tail. On every get or put the accessed node is moved to the head, and when capacity is exceeded the tail node is evicted. Python's OrderedDict provides this exact behavior out of the box.
